metadata.source_authority: CBIC, GST Council 56th Meeting, Income Tax Act 1961
metadata.disclaimer: Information is subject to change. Verify with official portals (gst.gov.in, ewaybillgst.gov.in).

due_dates.qrmp_iff: 13th of next month (for M1 & M2 of quarter)

eligibility_conditions: Possession of valid tax invoice/debit note.
Supplier has filed GSTR-1/IFF and paid tax.
ITC is reflected in GSTR-2B (Mandatory since Feb 2025).
blocked_credits_section_17_5: Motor vehicles (seating capacity <= 13) unless used for further supply...
Works contract services for construction of immovable property...
Goods/services for construction of immovable property on own account.
Goods lost, stolen, destroyed, written off, or disposed of by way of gift/free samples.
Tax paid under composition scheme, fraud, or willful misstatement.

faqs.question: My supplier's invoice is not showing in GSTR-2B. Can I claim ITC?
faqs.answer: No. As per Rule 60 amendment (Feb 2025), GSTR-2B is the mandatory basis for ITC claims. Contact your supplier to file their GSTR-1/IFF.

faqs.question: How much late fee for GSTR-3B 10 days late with tax liability?
faqs.answer: For returns with tax liability, late fee is ₹50 per day. For 10 days: ₹500. Cap is ₹5,000. Plus 18% p.a. interest on unpaid tax.
